entries.name: Vertical Bar Chart
entries.encoding: Position along x-axis maps category; height maps value.

stacks.vega-lite: mark: bar with x: nominal, y: quantitative

a11y: always include axis labels and units
pair with a data table for screen readers
use sufficient bar-to-gap ratio (>=0.4)

entries.name: Horizontal Bar Chart
when_to_use: ranked lists where ordering is the story
when_to_skip: very short labels that fit beneath vertical bars
entries.encoding: Position along y-axis maps category; width maps value.

stacks.d3: d3.scaleBand on y + rect with computed width
stacks.vega-lite: mark: bar with y: nominal, x: quantitative

a11y: sort descending unless ordering carries meaning
show exact values on bars when space permits
ensure label truncation has a tooltip

entries.name: Grouped Bar Chart
when_to_skip: if part-to-whole is the actual story (use stacked)
entries.encoding: Outer position maps category; inner offset maps series; height maps value.

stacks.vega-lite: xOffset channel for series grouping

a11y: use a categorical palette that is colorblind safe
pattern fills as a secondary encoding for B/W printing
legend order matches bar order within group

entries.name: Stacked Bar Chart
when_to_skip: comparing individual segment values across bars (hard to read)
entries.encoding: Segments stack vertically (or horizontally); total height encodes sum, segment heights encode parts.

stacks.vega-lite: y: aggregate sum with color: nominal
stacks.chartjs: type: 'bar' with stacked: true on both scales

a11y: 100% stacked variant should be labeled as percentages
always include legend with consistent ordering
consider a paired view (stacked + small multiples) for accurate comparison

entries.name: Single Line Chart
when_to_use: long time ranges with many points
entries.encoding: Connected points encode value over an ordered axis (usually time).

stacks.vega-lite: mark: line with x: temporal, y: quantitative

a11y: mark notable inflection points with annotations
include date range in chart title
use line width >= 2px for low-vision readers

entries.name: Multi-Line Chart
when_to_skip: more than 7 series (use small multiples instead)
series with vastly different scales (use dual-axis or normalize)
entries.encoding: Multiple lines on a shared axis; color encodes series identity.

stacks.d3: d3.line() per series mapped over data array
stacks.vega-lite: mark: line with color: nominal channel

a11y: direct-label lines at end rather than a separate legend
use distinguishable line styles (dashed, dotted) as secondary encoding
ensure at least 3:1 contrast between adjacent series

entries.name: Area Chart
when_to_skip: multiple overlapping series (filled areas occlude each other)
values that go negative across zero
entries.encoding: Line with the region between line and baseline filled.

stacks.vega-lite: mark: area with x: temporal, y: quantitative

a11y: use a fill opacity around 0.2-0.4 so the line stays visible
avoid heavy gradients that obscure values
always show y-axis baseline

entries.name: Stacked Area Chart
when_to_skip: data with gaps or missing points
entries.encoding: Areas stack on top of each other; total height = sum; each band = one series.

stacks.vega-lite: mark: area with color: nominal and stack channel
stacks.chartjs: type: 'line' with fill: 'origin' and stacked scales

a11y: use a 100% normalized variant when proportions matter more than totals
order series by stability (least-varying at bottom)
label each band directly when possible

entries.name: Pie Chart
when_to_use: executive summary contexts where comparison is informal
when_to_skip: any case where a bar chart would also work
entries.encoding: Angle of each slice encodes proportion of the whole.

stacks.vega-lite: mark: arc with theta: quantitative

a11y: always show percentages or values as labels
do not use as the primary chart for decision-making
pair with a data table when 4+ slices exist

entries.name: Donut Chart
when_to_use: same as pie but with center space for a total or KPI
entries.encoding: Annular ring with angle encoding proportion; center reserved for a total or KPI.

stacks.vega-lite: mark: arc with innerRadius

a11y: center total must have clear semantic markup, not visually-only
labels follow same rules as pie
ensure ring thickness is at least 16px at intended display size

entries.name: Scatter Plot
when_to_skip: more than 5000 points without aggregation (use density plot)
entries.encoding: Position (x, y) encodes two quantitative values; optionally color, shape, or size encode additional dimensions.

stacks.d3: circle elements with cx, cy bound to data
stacks.vega-lite: mark: point with x and y both quantitative

a11y: use markers with at least 2px stroke for outline definition
describe correlation direction in chart caption
consider regression-line overlay for trend communication

entries.name: Bubble Chart
when_to_use: three quantitative dimensions on one chart
when_to_skip: fewer than 5 data points (no advantage over a table)
if the third dimension is binary
entries.encoding: x and y position plus circle area encode three quantitative values.

stacks.d3: circle with r bound to a sqrt scale of the third dimension
stacks.vega-lite: mark: point with size channel
stacks.chartjs: type: 'bubble' with r in data points

a11y: never map size linearly to value (perception is by area, so use sqrt scale)
minimum bubble diameter 8px for distinguishability
legend must show size-to-value mapping with sample bubbles

entries.name: Heatmap
when_to_use: matrix of values across two categorical dimensions
when_to_skip: sparse data with many empty cells
if precise values matter more than patterns
entries.encoding: Color intensity encodes value at the intersection of two categorical axes.

stacks.d3: rect grid with sequential color scale
stacks.recharts: no native heatmap; compose with rect or grid library
stacks.vega-lite: mark: rect with x and y nominal, color quantitative

a11y: never use rainbow palettes; use perceptually uniform (viridis, magma)
include numeric values on hover and on focus
provide a sequential legend with min/max anchored

entries.name: Calendar Heatmap
entries.encoding: Each cell is a date in a year-grid; color encodes daily aggregate value.

stacks.d3: d3.timeDays + rect grid with weekday/week scales
stacks.recharts: no native; compose with grid library
stacks.vega-lite: mark: rect with x: day, y: week, color: quantitative

a11y: each cell must be focusable with date + value in accessible name
use ordinal sequential palette (5-7 stops)
include month-boundary visual markers

entries.name: Histogram
when_to_use: distribution of a single continuous variable
entries.encoding: X-axis binned into continuous ranges; bar height encodes count per bin.

stacks.vega-lite: mark: bar with x: bin: true

a11y: bin count justification belongs in the chart caption
axes labels must clarify bin widths and units
consider a paired density overlay for shape clarity

entries.name: Box Plot
when_to_skip: very small sample sizes (under 10 per box)
entries.encoding: Box spans IQR (25th-75th percentile); median line inside; whiskers extend to range; points show outliers.

stacks.d3: compose rect + line + circle from pre-computed quantiles
stacks.recharts: no native; compose ErrorBar with Bar
stacks.vega-lite: mark: boxplot with x: nominal, y: quantitative

a11y: always annotate min, Q1, median, Q3, max in caption or tooltip
use a single color when comparing across one dimension
describe boxplot anatomy near first use in interface

entries.name: Violin Plot
entries.encoding: Mirrored kernel density estimate around a vertical axis; width encodes frequency at each value.

stacks.d3: compose path with d3.area + KDE smoothing
stacks.recharts: no native; build with path primitives
stacks.vega-lite: transform: density + mark: area mirrored

a11y: include median markers inside the violin
explain the encoding in the legend or caption
consider a boxplot overlay for non-technical readers

entries.name: Candlestick (OHLC) Chart
entries.encoding: Body spans open-to-close; wicks extend to high and low; color indicates up (close>open) vs down.

stacks.d3: compose rect for body + line for wick per period
stacks.vega-lite: mark: rule + bar with combined encodings

a11y: green/red is conventional but never the only encoding (add shape or pattern)
include explicit OHLC values in tooltip
time-axis labels must include timezone

entries.name: Waterfall Chart
when_to_use: showing incremental contributions to a total
entries.encoding: Bars stacked sequentially; each starts where the previous ended; color encodes positive vs negative contribution.

stacks.d3: compute cumulative offsets then render rects
stacks.recharts: compose <BarChart> with stacked bars and computed base
stacks.vega-lite: transform: window + mark: bar with computed y2
stacks.chartjs: type: 'bar' with floating bars (y as [start, end])

a11y: label every bar with its delta value
show the running total at each step
color encoding must have a pattern alternative for printing

entries.name: Funnel Chart
entries.encoding: Stages stacked vertically; width at each stage encodes value (typically count or rate).

stacks.d3: polygon paths between paired x-coordinates per stage
stacks.vega-lite: mark: trail with custom width encoding

a11y: always show absolute counts and conversion rate at each step
label each stage clearly on the funnel itself
use sequential color steps that imply progression

entries.name: Sankey Diagram
when_to_use: flow between categories with conserved totals
entries.encoding: Nodes connected by bands; band width encodes flow magnitude between source and target.

stacks.vega-lite: no first-class support; use Vega specification

a11y: label both nodes and key flows directly
use categorical color on source nodes that propagates to bands
provide tabular flow summary as alternative view

entries.name: Parallel Coordinates
when_to_use: filtering and brushing in exploration tools
entries.encoding: Each dimension is a vertical axis; each record is a polyline crossing all axes at its values.

stacks.d3: d3.line() across mapped axes per record
stacks.recharts: no native; build with custom path
stacks.vega-lite: transform: fold + mark: line with detail channel
stacks.chartjs: no native; use d3 inside chart shell

a11y: brushing must have keyboard equivalents
axis order is a design decision; explain rationale
highlight selected polylines with 3:1 contrast against unselected

entries.name: Radar (Spider) Chart
entries.encoding: Polar layout with one axis per dimension; closed polygon connects values along each axis.

stacks.d3: polygon path with polar coordinates per axis
stacks.vega-lite: no native; use Vega spec with polar projection

a11y: values do not encode area well; label all vertices with exact values
use semi-transparent fills (opacity around 0.3)
include grid rings labeled with reference values

entries.name: Treemap
when_to_skip: values that vary by orders of magnitude (small slices unreadable)
entries.encoding: Nested rectangles tile a containing area; rect area encodes value; nesting encodes hierarchy.

stacks.vega-lite: transform: treemap layout + mark: rect

a11y: each rect must have an accessible name with category and value
use ordinal color for top-level categories
avoid relying on text inside small rects (use tooltips)

entries.name: Sunburst Chart
when_to_skip: deep hierarchies (more than 4 levels become unreadable)
audiences who prefer treemaps for the same data
entries.encoding: Concentric rings represent levels of hierarchy; arc angle encodes value within each level.

stacks.recharts: no native; compose with custom arc generator
stacks.vega-lite: no native; use Vega partition transform
stacks.chartjs: no native; use d3 inside chart shell

a11y: interactive drill-down must work with keyboard
label each visible segment when space allows
provide a breadcrumb of current zoom path

entries.name: Gauge Chart
when_to_use: single KPI against a target or threshold
when_to_skip: comparing multiple metrics (gauges do not compare well)
any case where a labeled number would suffice
entries.encoding: Arc with a needle or filled segment; position along arc encodes value relative to range.

stacks.vega-lite: mark: arc with theta in a constrained range

a11y: always show the exact numeric value below or inside the gauge
include threshold markers labeled with their values
ensure colored zones are not the only signal (add labels)

entries.name: KPI Card
when_to_use: alongside a comparison delta and sparkline
when_to_skip: if multiple metrics need to be compared (use a table)
if the metric is meaningless without distribution context
entries.encoding: Large numeric value with a label; optional delta indicator and sparkline below.

stacks.recharts: compose <Sparkline> or <Line> inside a div card
stacks.vega-lite: compose mark: text with embedded mark: line
stacks.chartjs: compose type: 'line' minimal config inside a card

a11y: delta direction must use word or icon, not color alone
the metric label should be an h-tag with semantic value
currency or unit must be explicit, not inferred

entries.name: Sparkline
when_to_use: inline trend within a table cell or KPI card
when_to_skip: data spans wildly different magnitudes (lacks axis context)
entries.encoding: Small word-sized line chart without axes; emphasizes shape over exact values.

stacks.d3: d3.line() in a small SVG with no axes
stacks.vega-lite: mark: line with config: axis disabled
stacks.chartjs: type: 'line' with display: false on scales

a11y: include the latest value adjacent as text
provide a tooltip or table view for actual values
minimum line stroke 1.5px for visibility

entries.name: Density Plot
when_to_use: alternative to histogram for continuous data
entries.encoding: Smoothed kernel density estimate as a continuous curve; area under curve sums to 1.

stacks.vega-lite: transform: density + mark: area

a11y: disclose the bandwidth choice (it affects shape)
use semi-transparent fills when overlaying distributions
consider paired histogram for non-technical audiences

entries.name: Horizon Chart
entries.encoding: Series mirrored and banded by intensity; color band plus density encode magnitude and sign.

stacks.vega-lite: supported via mark: rect with layered transforms
stacks.chartjs: no native; use d3 inside chart shell

a11y: always pair with a table view
use ordinal palette, never rainbow
include band-width explanation near first use

entries.name: Ridgeline Plot
when_to_use: distributions across many ordered categories (e.g. months)
when_to_skip: categories that are not naturally ordered
entries.encoding: Stacked, partially overlapping density curves; vertical offset encodes category; ridge height encodes density.

stacks.d3: compose d3.area() per row with KDE
stacks.recharts: no native; build with path primitives
stacks.vega-lite: facet on y with mark: area + offset transform
stacks.chartjs: no native; use d3 inside chart shell

a11y: label each ridge directly at the start of the row
use sequential color or single color across all ridges
include median markers per ridge

entries.name: Bullet Chart
when_to_use: progress to target with qualitative bands
when_to_skip: comparing many entities (better as a sortable table)
entries.encoding: Horizontal bar with feature measure plus comparative target marker against qualitative range bands.

stacks.d3: compose nested rects + line for target
stacks.vega-lite: layered mark: bar + tick
stacks.chartjs: no native; compose via bar with custom plugin

a11y: always label the target value clearly
use 3 qualitative bands max (bad/ok/good)
include exact measure value as text alongside

entries.name: Choropleth Map
when_to_use: regional comparisons of a single metric
election results, population density, regional KPIs
when_to_skip: absolute counts where area bias distorts perception
comparing very small regions adjacent to very large ones
entries.encoding: Geographic regions filled with sequential color encoding a quantitative value.

stacks.vega-lite: mark: geoshape with color: quantitative

a11y: always include a sortable data table as an alternative view
use perceptually uniform palettes (viridis, blues)
label key regions directly when space allows

entries.name: Network Graph
when_to_use: social networks, dependency graphs, knowledge graphs
when_to_skip: more than 200 nodes without aggregation (hairball problem)
entries.encoding: Nodes as circles connected by edges; force-directed or hierarchical layout encodes structure.

stacks.vega-lite: no native; use Vega force directives
stacks.chartjs: no native; use d3 or cytoscape.js inside chart shell

a11y: provide a tabular adjacency view as alternative
ensure node selection is keyboard accessible
highlight neighborhoods on hover and focus consistently

entries.name: Dual-Axis Mixed Chart
when_to_use: two related metrics with different scales over time
entries.encoding: Shared x-axis; primary y-axis maps one metric (e.g. bars), secondary y-axis maps another (e.g. line).

stacks.d3: compose two scales with overlaid mark renderers
stacks.vega-lite: layer spec with independent y-resolutions

a11y: label both y-axes with their units explicitly
use color to associate each series with its axis
include a disclaimer when axes use independent scales
